results.title: Assoc Staff Accountant - BSC (Hybrid) | Hirevector
highlights:
Assoc Staff Accountant - BSC (Hybrid) | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Assoc Staff Accountant - BSC (Hybrid)

Posted May 27, 2026

Exelon is leading the energy transformation and is seeking an Assoc Staff Accountant. The role involves performing various accounting assignments, recording financial transactions, and ensuring compliance with Sarbanes-Oxley controls while collaborating with cross-functional teams.

### Responsibilities

- Perform and support the monthly financial statement close process, ensuring that all financial transactions are recorded accurately and timely
- Ensure effective operation of Sarbanes-Oxley controls and identify any changes that may be necessary
- Prepare monthly and quarterly reports, including analysis for use in internal and external reporting
- Research variances, identify trends in data (at times working with large data sets), determine root causes and make recommendations for adjustments and/or enhancements
- Prepare and analyze account reconciliations
- Engage and collaborate with cross-functional business partners to identify and resolve accounting issues proactively and to assist with planning, forecasting, regulatory, and business decision processes
- Present and communicate operating results as needed to Leadership
- Interact with internal and external auditors to support required audits
- Support the regulatory process, including various data requests, which could include interaction with external stakeholders
- Analyze current processes and procedures and leverage new and emerging technologies to recommend, develop, and drive improvements
- Participate in various special projects related to process improvements, automation, and/or changes in regulatory or accounting requirements

Skills

- Bachelor's degree in Accounting or equivalent
- Entry level, no years of related experience required
- Knowledge and understanding of Accounting theory, principles, practices and regulations including FASB/GAAP and SOX compliance
- Ability to document a broad range of issues in work papers, disclosures and memos
- Strong analytical skills in evaluating data and the ability to exercise judgment in a variety of areas
- Effective teambuilding and leadership skills
- Project management skills
- Commitment to highest standards of quality and integrity
- Exude a high degree of professionalism and treats others with respect
- MBA with a concentration in Accounting or Finance
- CPA
- Public accounting experience
- Regulatory/utility/FERC/SEC reporting or energy industry experience
- Understanding of the use and implementation of analytical tools and artificial intelligence to improve processes and analysis
- Demonstrated successful use of technology to improve process efficiency

### Benefits

- Annual Bonus for eligible positions: 7%
- 401(k) match and annual company contribution
- Medical, dental and vision insurance
- Life and disability insurance
- Generous paid time off options, including vacation, sick time, floating and fixed holidays, maternity leave and bonding/primary caregiver leave or parental leave
- Employee Assistance Program and resources for mental and emotional support
- Wellbeing programs such as tuition reimbursement, adoption and surrogacy assistance and fitness reimbursement
- Referral bonus program
- And much more

Company Overview

- Exelon Corporation is a utility services holding company that provides services for energy generation businesses in the United States. It was founded in 1999, and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.exeloncorp.com/. Company H1B Sponsorship

- Exelon has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 8 in 2025, 7 in 2024, 5 in 2023, 6 in 2022, 4 in 2021, 1 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Apply for This Job

# Principal Organizational Change Management Consultant job at Exelon in Oakbrook Terrace, IL, Baltimore, MD, Chicago, IL, Washington, DC, Newark, DE, Philadelphia, PA
[...]
Exelon is strengthening its enterprise Organizational Change Management capability to ensure employees and leaders are equipped to navigate ongoing organizational change successfully.

The Principal Change Management Consultant assigned to projects supports the framework used to proactively plan and manage the steps required to prepare impacted business areas for change. This role contributes to the design and implementation of project change deliverables including change plans, communication, stakeholder engagement, feedback mechanisms, and training activities. This role provides advisory support to project resources on the application of change management.

This role provides advisory support and research to advance the organization's change management practice. The Consultant contributes to the design and enhancement of enterprise OCM training and capability building resources that strengthen organizational change leadership capability.

Able to simplify the complex, making it easy for people to understand the changes, what they need to do, and how to succeed.Excellent active listening skills, adapts communications style as needed to best suit the audience and situation. Agile, with a learning mindset.Strong partnering and influencing skills with a demonstrated ability to think strategically and systemically and achieve results collaborating across organizational boundaries.Demonstrated ability to operate in a fast, complex, and changing environment.

[...]
• Own and deliver a portfolio of AI and analytics initiatives that measurably improve sales, operations, and customer experience. • Translate business problems into actionable data products (dashboards, models, automations, and self-service insights). • Partner with stakeholders to define success metrics, prioritize use cases, and drive adoption through change management and enablement. • Establish scalable, governed data pipelines that improve data quality, availability, and trust across WPB. • Lead the identification, evaluation, and implementation of AI capabilities to optimize business processes, improve forecast accuracy, increase targeted sales, and enhance supply chain and operational performance. • Build and maintain executive-ready analytics and reporting (e.g., Power BI) that translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. • Design, implement, and operate scalable data pipelines and data products using Databricks and related tooling; ensure data quality, lineage, and reliability. • Partner with business leaders and SMEs to discover high-value use cases, define requirements, establish success metrics, and drive adoption. • Evaluate vendor and internal AI solutions (including GenAI) for fit, risk, scalability, and ROI; recommend a path forward and lead pilots/proof-of-value efforts. • Establish lightweight AI governance practices (data privacy, responsible use, model performance monitoring) aligned to company standards and a regulated environment. • Deliver and improve internal and external digital experiences (websites and B2B marketplace platforms) that support customer needs, compliance, and brand quality. • Apply agile project management practices to plan work, manage dependencies, and deliver incremental value on a predictable cadence. • Create documentation, plans, and training that enable self-service analytics and improve data literacy across the organization. • Provide quality assurance and testing support for new analytics, automation, and AI-related tools to ensure stability and positive user experience.

highlights:
# Senior AI Agent Engineer
[...]
- We need 5+ years of relevant experience, along with a Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, AI Engineering, or a related discipline. - We need 3+ years of experience developing and deploying AI or GenAI applications, especially autonomous agent systems or similar AI software engineering work. - We need 3+ years of experience building GenAI solutions that support agent orchestration, multi-step reasoning, prompt design, RAG integration, and model selection. - We need 3+ years of experience working with LLMs and deep learning models, including the machine learning lifecycle, data generation methods, training, and validation. - We need strong programming ability in Python and/or languages such as JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, Java, or Go, with solid software engineering practices. - We need hands-on expertise with AI and ML frameworks and tools such as LangChain, OpenAI APIs, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and commercial or open-source LLMs. - We need practical knowledge of prompt engineering, natural language processing, multi-agent systems, and orchestration platforms such as AutoGen, LangGraph, or MCP. - We need familiarity with data management, vector databases, semantic search, and real-time data pipelines. - We need experience deploying AI solutions on cloud environments such as AWS or Google Cloud using Docker and Kubernetes. - We need a strong understanding of model training, fine-tuning, evaluation, AI ethics, data privacy, and secure information handling. - We need the ability to work independently in fast-paced, experimental settings. - We require applicants to be 18 years or older and legally authorized to work in the United States. - We require candidates to be willing to work from our Niskayuna, NY or Cambridge, MA office locations.

- We design, build, and tune AI agents using LLMs, reinforcement learning, planning methods, and decision-making frameworks. - We develop scalable multi-agent systems that support long-horizon reasoning, autonomy, interaction, and complex task execution. - We connect AI agents with APIs, backend systems, databases, and enterprise platforms. - We prototype, launch, and support AI-powered solutions while maintaining reliability and performance in production. - We improve agent behavior through ongoing feedback loops, reinforcement learning, and user input. - We work closely with research, engineering, product, and deployment teams to refine capabilities and drive innovation. - We evaluate agent performance, run structured testing, add safety controls, and uphold responsible AI practices. - We document architectures, design choices, workflows, and technical processes thoroughly. - We stay informed about new AI developments, contribute to platform and tooling enhancements, and share knowledge across our team. - We push forward the boundaries of planning, reasoning, and agentic intelligence through large-scale AI and ML solutions.

results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
[...]
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date May 15, 2026
[...]
## Job Details
[...]
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
[...]
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
[...]
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
[...]
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
[...]
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
[...]
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
[...]
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
[...]
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
[...]
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
[...]
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
[...]
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
[...]
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
[...]
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
[...]
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
[...]
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
[...]
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
[...]
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
[...]
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
[...]
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems
[...]
$181,500 - $283,800

# Principal AI Solutions Architect
[...]
The nuclear industry is undergoing a digital renaissance, and we are looking for a Principal AI Solutions Architect to lead the charge. This isn't your standard enterprise cloud role. You will design high-stakes AI systems that must balance the latest innovation with the most stringent safety and security requirements on the planet.
[...]
Working remotely, you will report to the Manager, Digital Security & Architecture. The ideal candidate has a "Nuclear-Grade" mindset for compliance and reliability, combined with the agility to understand the "Security for AI" revolution.
[...]
- Design and implement enterprise-scale GCP platforms (Google SecOps/Mandiant)
- Create and deploy "Agentic Security" layers, using LLMs and Vertex AI to automate detection engineering and investigation lifecycles.
[...]
- Translate complex regulatory requirements (
[...]
into actionable technical controls.
[...]
- Ensure all AI solutions adhere to global data privacy standards (GDPR/SOC2) and implement "Guardrail" systems to prevent AI hallucinations or data leakage.
[...]
- Ensure that the GCP platform follows Westinghouse security standards
[...]
- Improve standards for model versioning, automated testing, and deployment (CI/CD) to ensure models are updated without downtime.
[...]
- Infrastructure & Cost: Architect "Right-sized" compute solutions (GPU/TPU) and implement strategies to minimize token costs and inference latency.
[...]
- Design deep-learning architectures for "Zero-Fail" sensor monitoring across primary and secondary containment systems.
[...]
- 10+ Years of Cyber Experience: A proven track record spanning Managed Security Services (MSSP), Nuclear/Industrial Control Systems (ICS), and Cloud Security.
[...]
- Deep Technical Pedigree: Extensive hands-on experience with the Google Cloud Security stack (SecOps, SCC, Gemini for Security, Model Armor) or equivalent enterprise ecosystems (Mandiant/FireEye/Palo Alto).
[...]
- OT/ICS Expertise: Experience securing air-gapped environments and "live-plant" operational requirements for critical infrastructure.
[...]
- AI/ML Fluency: Demonstrated ability to secure AI workloads and use AI to reduce Mean Time to Respond (MTTR).

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Translate biological and chemical domain
[...]
into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7): 5+ years of industry or post-PhD experience with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,800 of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.

Genentech is a biotechnology research company that specializes in genetic testing and personalized medicines.

# Senior Lead AI Engineer(MLX, Agentic AI, Gen AI platform Services)
[...]
Lead AI engineer role at Capital One's Intelligent Foundations and Experiences team, designing and optimizing large-scale LLM systems, foundation models, and agentic AI platforms for production banking applications.
[...]
At Capital One, we are creating responsible and reliable AI systems, changing banking for good. For years, Capital One has been an industry leader in using machine learning to create real-time, personalized customer experiences. Our investments in technology infrastructure and world-class talent — along with our deep experience in machine learning — position us to be at the forefront of enterprises leveraging AI. From informing customers about unusual charges to answering their questions in real time, our applications of AI & ML are bringing humanity and simplicity to banking. We are committed to continuing to build world-class applied science and engineering teams to deliver our industry leading capabilities with breakthrough product experiences and scalable, high-performance AI infrastructure. At Capital One, you will help bring the transformative power of emerging AI capabilities to reimagine how we serve our customers and businesses who have come to love the products and services we build.
[...]
The Intelligent Foundations and Experiences (IFX) team is at the center of bringing our vision for AI at Capital One to life. We work hand-in-hand with our partners across the company to advance the state of the art in science and AI engineering, and we build and deploy proprietary solutions that are central to our business and deliver value to millions of customers. Our AI models and platforms empower teams across Capital One to enhance their products with the transformative power of AI, in responsible and scalable ways for the highest leverage impact.

Experience developing and applying state-of-the-art techniques for optimizing training and inference software to improve hardware utilization, latency, throughput, and cost

Passion for staying abreast of the latest AI research and AI systems, and judiciously apply novel techniques in production

## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)

A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. [...]
The AI Platform, Principal Engineer is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining robust AI platforms that support advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence applications. This role works closely with data scientists, software engineers, and product managers to develop scalable solutions that enhance the efficiency, reliability, and performance of our AI systems.
[...]
- Design and develop scalable AI platforms to support various machine learning and deep learning models.
[...]
- Collaborate with data scientists to understand model requirements and optimize infrastructure accordingly.
[...]
- Implement and maintain data pipelines, model deployment strategies, and monitoring systems.
[...]
- Ensure high availability and reliability of AI services through effective troubleshooting and performance tuning.
[...]
Stay updated with industry trends
[...]
- Stay current with the latest advancements in AI and machine learning technologies and incorporate them into the platform.
[...]
guidance and support to
[...]
- Proven experience in building and maintaining AI or machine learning platforms.
[...]
- Excellent understanding NLP, and AI/ML design and implementation concepts
[...]
relational mapping
[...]
- Experience with machine learning frameworks and libraries such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn
[...]
# Preferred Qualifications
[...]
- Advanced degree in computer science, data science, AI, or related field.
- Experience with big data technologies (Hadoop, Spark).
- Experience with reinforcement learning, generative AI, or autonomous systems.
- Knowledge of MLOps practices and tools.
- Knowledge of insurance-specific AI applications.
- Hands-on experience working with AI/ML as a Data Scientist.
- Experience in deploying machine learning models in production environments.
- Understanding of distributed systems and microservices architecture.

results.title: Exelon Positioned For Capturing Consequential Growth And AI Tailwinds - Cyber Media Creations
highlights:
Exelon is strategically positioned to benefit from upcoming growth opportunities and artificial intelligence tailwinds, according to recent analysis.
[...]
Exelon is preparing to capitalize on significant growth opportunities and the rising influence of AI. This development is based on strategic positioning and market analysis. The company’s future growth prospects depend on execution amid evolving industry trends.
[...]
Exelon is strategically positioned to capture significant growth opportunities and benefit from artificial intelligence tailwinds, according to recent market analysis.
[...]
Exelon, a major player in the energy sector, has undertaken strategic initiatives aimed at expanding its market share and investing in emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence. While specific financial details are not disclosed, analysts suggest that the company’s focus on modernization and innovation could lead to substantial growth in the coming years.
[...]
Recent reports indicate that Exelon is increasing investments in smart grid technology and AI-driven energy management systems, which could enhance operational efficiency and customer service. These initiatives align with broader industry trends toward digital transformation and sustainable energy solutions.
[...]
This development matters because it signals Exelon’s intent to leverage technological advancements for competitive advantage, potentially boosting shareholder value and industry leadership. As the energy sector undergoes rapid transformation, companies that effectively integrate AI and modernize infrastructure are likely to outperform peers. For investors, this suggests a promising outlook for Exelon’s stock and long-term growth prospects.
[...]
Exelon has historically been a significant utility provider in the United States, with a focus on nuclear, renewable, and traditional energy sources. Over recent years, the company has shifted toward modernization, including investments in digital infrastructure and sustainable energy. Industry analysts have increasingly highlighted AI as a key driver of efficiency and innovation in energy management, which Exelon appears to be actively pursuing.
[...]
“Exelon’s strategic focus on AI and modernization positions it well to capitalize on future growth opportunities in the evolving energy landscape.”
[...]
“Our investments in digital technology and AI are designed to enhance our operational efficiency and deliver better value to our customers.”
[...]
It is not yet clear how quickly Exelon will realize these growth benefits or how the market will respond to its technological investments. Additionally, the full scope and scale of AI integration remain to be seen, and external factors such as regulatory changes could influence outcomes.
[...]
Exelon is expected to continue its investments in digital infrastructure and AI, with upcoming quarterly reports providing insight into the financial impact of these initiatives. Industry observers will monitor the company’s progress toward achieving its strategic goals over the next 12-18 months.
[...]
### What specific growth opportunities is Exelon pursuing?
[...]
Exelon is focusing on expanding its market share through modernization initiatives, investing in smart grid technology, and deploying AI-driven energy management systems to improve efficiency and customer service.
[...]
### How will AI benefit Exelon’s operations?
[...]
AI is expected to enhance operational efficiency, optimize energy distribution, reduce costs, and improve predictive maintenance, giving Exelon a competitive edge in the energy sector.
[...]
Results may become evident within the next 12-18 months, as the company integrates AI into its operations and scales its modernization efforts, with quarterly reports providing updates on progress.
